 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","title":"P2.2 Indexer multi-root orchestration","description":"# Indexer Multi-Root Orchestration

## Context
The indexer needs to build a list of scan roots and orchestrate scanning across them, injecting provenance appropriately.

## Location
src/indexer/mod.rs

## Current Flow
1. Build single ScanContext with data_dir
2. For each connector, call scan()
3. Persist results

## New Flow
1. Build list of scan roots:
   - Local roots (from watch_roots() or equivalent)
   - Remote mirror roots (from sources table)
2. Group roots by connector affinity
3. For each connector:
   - For each relevant root:
     - Create ScanContext with that root
     - Call scan()
     - Inject provenance into results
     - Persist with source_id

## Implementation

### Build Scan Roots List
\`\`\`rust
fn build_scan_roots(storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Vec<ScanRoot> {
    let mut roots = Vec::new();
    
    // Add local roots (existing logic from watch_roots())
    for local_root in local_default_roots() {
        roots.push(ScanRoot {
            path: local_root,
            origin: Origin::local(),
            platform: Some(Platform::current()),
            workspace_rewrites: vec![],
        });
    }
    
    // Add remote mirror roots
    for source in storage.list_sources()? {
        if source.kind == SourceKind::Ssh {
            let mirror_root = data_dir.join("remotes").join(&source.id).join("mirror");
            if mirror_root.exists() {
                roots.push(ScanRoot {
                    path: mirror_root,
                    origin: Origin {
                        source_id: source.id.clone(),
                        kind: source.kind,
                        host: source.host_label.clone(),
                    },
                    platform: source.platform,
                    workspace_rewrites: parse_rewrites(&source.config),
                });
            }
        }
    }
    
    roots
}
\`\`\`

### Connector Affinity
Not all roots apply to all connectors. For example:
- ClaudeCodeConnector cares about ~/.claude or remotes/*/mirror/**/.claude
- AiderConnector cares about .aider.chat.history.md anywhere

For now: let connectors handle root relevance via their detect() logic.

### Provenance Injection
After connector.scan() returns:
\`\`\`rust
for conv in conversations.iter_mut() {
    conv.metadata["cass"] = json!({
        "origin": {
            "source_id": scan_root.origin.source_id,
            "kind": scan_root.origin.kind.as_str(),
            "host": scan_root.origin.host,
        }
    });
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
- P2.1 (ScanContext extended)
- P1.3 (storage accepts source_id)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Indexer builds list of scan roots
- [ ] Local roots work as before
- [ ] Provenance injected into metadata
- [ ] Results persisted with correct source_id
- [ ] Tests verify multi-root behavior","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:57:31.805169Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","close_reason":"Implemented multi-root orchestration with build_scan_roots(), inject_provenance(), and provenance extraction in persist::map_to_internal(). All 294 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1v7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:58:17.128097Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","title":"Task 4: Update template.rs and lib.rs integration","description":"# Objective
Update the HtmlExporter and lib.rs export flow to use the new MessageGroup system.

## Changes Required

### 1. HtmlExporter::export_messages signature update
In src/html_export/template.rs:

```rust
pub fn export_messages(
    &self,
    title: &str,
    groups: &[MessageGroup],  // Changed from &[Message]
    metadata: TemplateMetadata,
    password: Option<&str>,
) -> Result<String, TemplateError>
```

### 2. lib.rs run_export_html integration
Around line 10302, after creating Vec<Message>:

```rust
// OLD:
let messages: Vec<Message> = raw_messages.iter()...collect();

// NEW:
let flat_messages: Vec<Message> = raw_messages.iter()...collect();
let message_groups = group_messages_for_export(flat_messages);

// Update metadata message count
let metadata = TemplateMetadata {
    message_count: message_groups.len(), // or sum of all messages
    ...
};

// Pass groups to exporter
let html = exporter.export_messages(title, &message_groups, metadata, final_password.as_deref())?;
```

### 3. Update RenderOptions usage
Ensure render options properly flow through to grouped rendering:
- show_tool_calls flag should control whether badges are shown
- show_timestamps applies to group primary message
- collapse_threshold applies to group primary content

### 4. Backwards Compatibility
We do NOT care about backwards compatibility (per AGENTS.md). Change the API directly, update all call sites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] export_messages accepts MessageGroup slice
- [ ] lib.rs calls grouping function before export
- [ ] Metadata reflects correct counts
- [ ] All compile errors resolved
- [ ] Integration works end-to-end","notes":"## Metadata Count Clarification

The TemplateMetadata.message_count field should reflect:
- **Total messages** (sum of all messages in all groups), NOT group count
- This maintains backwards compatibility with existing exports
- Add new field if needed: group_count

```rust
let metadata = TemplateMetadata {
    message_count: message_groups.iter()
        .map(|g| 1 + g.tool_calls.len()) // primary + tools
        .sum(),
    // Or add: group_count: message_groups.len(),
    ...
};
```

## CLI Flag Behavior

### --include-tools flag
- TRUE (default): Show tool badges in message headers
- FALSE: Hide all tool badges, show only message content

### --show-timestamps flag
- Applies to group primary message timestamp
- Tool individual timestamps not shown (too noisy)

### --no-cdns flag
- Unchanged behavior
- All CSS/JS still inlined when CDNs disabled

### --theme flag
- Unchanged: "dark" or "light" default
- Applied to grouped messages same as before

## OpenCode Session Handling

The existing `load_opencode_session_for_export()` returns raw messages.
These still need to go through the grouping function:

```rust
if is_opencode {
    match load_opencode_session_for_export(session_path) {
        Ok((title, start, end, msgs)) => {
            // msgs are raw serde_json::Value, convert to Message first
            let flat_messages = msgs.iter()
                .filter_map(|m| convert_opencode_message(m))
                .collect();
            let message_groups = group_messages_for_export(flat_messages, AgentFormat::OpenCode);
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

## Error Handling

If grouping fails (shouldn't, but for safety):
```rust
let message_groups = group_messages_for_export(flat_messages, agent_format);
if message_groups.is_empty() && !flat_messages.is_empty() {
    warn!(
        flat_count = flat_messages.len(),
        "Grouping produced no groups from non-empty messages, falling back"
    );
    // Fallback: each message becomes its own group
    message_groups = flat_messages.into_iter()
        .map(|m| MessageGroup::new(m, MessageGroupType::from_role(&m.role)))
        .collect();
}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:57:11.883247813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:17:09.152702697Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:17:09.152606859Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","depends_on_id":"2jxn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:57:11.898052203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1v5c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T22:06:40.683240667Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-27t2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:57:21.105707714Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1ohe","title":"[Epic] E2E Verbose Logging Enhancement","description":"## Epic: E2E Verbose Logging Enhancement

Enhance E2E test logging beyond basic JSONL events to include detailed debugging information, performance metrics, and failure context.

### Goals
1. **Every test failure is debuggable from logs alone** - No need to re-run locally
2. **Performance regressions are detectable** - Timing data for critical operations
3. **Resource leaks are traceable** - Memory, file handles, temp files

### Current State
- Basic JSONL events: run_start, test_start, test_end, run_end
- Missing: verbose step-by-step output, metrics, failure state dumps

### Features in This Epic

1. **Verbose Debug Logging Mode** (395v)
   - `E2E_VERBOSE=1` environment variable
   - Detailed step-by-step logs to separate file
   - Does not affect JSONL event stream

2. **Performance Metrics Collection** (1bqi)
   - Emit `metrics` events with timing data
   - Track: indexer duration, search latency, memory usage, index size

3. **Failure State Dump** (rtpd)
   - On panic/failure, automatically dump:
     - Environment variables
     - Temp directory listing
     - Last 100 lines of logs
     - Database state

### Success Metrics
- 100% of test failures can be triaged from CI logs
- P95 operation times available in test-results/
- Resource leak reports generated automatically","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:23:59.502864770Z","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:23:34.832703992Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:23:34.832529397Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -115,7 +115,7 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jpl","title":"Add metrics to e2e_cli_flows.rs","description":"## Priority 2: Add Performance Metrics to e2e_cli_flows.rs

### Current State
tests/e2e_cli_flows.rs uses PhaseTracker but doesn't emit performance metrics.

### Required Changes

1. **Add E2ePerformanceMetrics import:**
```rust
use util::e2e_log::{..., E2ePerformanceMetrics};
```

2. **Capture timing for each CLI command:**
```rust
let start = Instant::now();
let output = Command::new(cass_bin())
    .args(&["search", "authentication", "--robot", "--limit", "10"])
    .output()?;
let duration = start.elapsed();

tracker.metrics("cass_search", &E2ePerformanceMetrics {
    duration_ms: duration.as_millis() as u64,
    items_processed: Some(parse_result_count(&output.stdout)),
    memory_bytes: None,
    throughput_per_sec: None,
});
```

### Suggested Metrics
| Command | Metric Name | What to Capture |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| cass search | cass_search | duration, result_count |
| cass index | cass_index | duration, sessions_indexed |
| cass view | cass_view | duration |
| cass stats | cass_stats | duration |
| cass health | cass_health | duration |

### Files to Modify
- tests/e2e_cli_flows.rs

### Testing Requirements (CRITICAL)

1. **Verify metrics in JSONL:**
```bash
E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --test e2e_cli_flows -- --nocapture
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics" and .test.suite == "e2e_cli_flows")'
```

2. **Verify metrics have required fields:**
```bash
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics") | {name, duration_ms: .metrics.duration_ms, items: .metrics.items_processed}'
```

3. **Verify metrics align with schema:**
```bash
# All metrics must have: name, metrics.duration_ms
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics" and (.metrics.duration_ms == null or .name == null)) | "INVALID: \(.)"'
# Should return empty
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Key CLI commands have timing metrics
- [ ] items_processed captured for search commands
- [ ] Metrics follow E2ePerformanceMetrics schema
- [ ] All metrics appear in JSONL output
- [ ] Metrics values are reasonable (not 0, not negative)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:20:39.226119058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:52:18.375163289Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:52:18.375096114Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added E2ePerformanceMetrics to setup_indexed_env (cass_index with throughput), search_basic (cass_search with hit count), view_command (cass_view), health_command (cass_health), stats_command (cass_stats)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jxn","title":"EPIC: HTML Export Complete Redesign - Message Grouping + Premium Styling","description":"# Context & Problem Statement

The HTML export feature has a FUNDAMENTAL structural flaw: tool calls and tool_results render as SEPARATE message bubbles instead of being consolidated as unobtrusive icons within their parent assistant message. Additionally, the visual styling is plain and doesn't match the premium Terminal Noir design system from the reference webapp.

## User Feedback (Verbatim)
- "ALL OF THOSE elements below the top message SHOULD BE SIMPLY ICONS WITHIN THE TOP MESSAGE IN THE UPPER RIGHT"
- "ZEROTH PERCENTILE DESIGN THAT LOOKS LIKE ABSOLUTE DOGSHIT"
- "Look at /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup... for how components are styled in an ultra slick, rich, attractive way"

## Reference Implementation
The Terminal Noir design system in /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/app/globals.css provides:
- Deep space palette with oklch colors
- Glassmorphism with backdrop-filter: blur(12px)
- Colored glow shadows
- Electric cyan primary (oklch 0.75 0.18 195)
- Fluid typography scale
- Responsive breakpoints

## Architecture Problem
Current flow in lib.rs run_export_html():
1. Each message from JSONL parsed into individual Message struct
2. No grouping - tool calls become separate messages
3. Renderer iterates individually, creating separate article elements

Required flow:
1. Parse JSONL messages
2. GROUP messages: assistant msg + following tool calls/results = single group
3. Render groups: tool calls become badges in parent message header

## Files to Modify
- src/lib.rs - Add message grouping logic before export
- src/html_export/renderer.rs - Update to handle grouped messages
- src/html_export/styles.rs - Premium glassmorphism styling

## Success Criteria
- Tool calls render as small icons in parent message header (upper-right)
- Hovering on tool badge shows details in popover
- Rich glassmorphism visual design matching reference
- Strong mobile/desktop differentiation
- Polished light AND dark themes
- All tests pass
- User signs off on visual appearance","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:55:26.170294412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:29:23.900567690Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:29:23.900491618Z","close_reason":"EPIC Complete - All 8 tasks finished: message grouping algorithm, rendering, tests, fixtures, validation","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2kio","title":"P6.5: Integration Tests","description":"# P6.5: Integration Tests

## Overview
End-to-end integration tests that verify the complete export -> encrypt -> bundle -> deploy -> decrypt -> search workflow works correctly across all supported scenarios.

## Test Scenarios

### 1) Full Export Pipeline (Password Only)
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_full_export_pipeline_password_only() {
    let temp_dir = tempdir().unwrap();
    let index = create_test_index(&temp_dir, 100).await;

    let output_dir = temp_dir.path().join("export");
    let result = export_to_ghpages(ExportConfig {
        index_path: index.path(),
        output_dir: output_dir.clone(),
        password: Some("test-password-123!".into()),
        recovery_secret: None,
        title: "Test Export".into(),
        ..Default::default()
    }).await;

    assert!(result.is_ok());

    // Verify bundle structure
    let site = output_dir.join("site");
    let private = output_dir.join("private");
    assert!(site.join("index.html").exists());
    assert!(site.join("sw.js").exists());
    assert!(site.join("config.json").exists());
    assert!(site.join("payload/chunk-00000.bin").exists());
    assert!(private.exists());

    // Verify config.json
    let config: ExportConfigJson = serde_json::from_str(
        &fs::read_to_string(site.join("config.json")).unwrap()
    ).unwrap();

    assert_eq!(config.key_slots.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(config.key_slots[0].kdf, "argon2id");
}
```

### 2) Full Export Pipeline (Password + Recovery)
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_full_export_pipeline_dual_auth() {
    let temp_dir = tempdir().unwrap();
    let index = create_test_index(&temp_dir, 50).await;

    let output_dir = temp_dir.path().join("export");
    let result = export_to_ghpages(ExportConfig {
        index_path: index.path(),
        output_dir: output_dir.clone(),
        password: Some("password123!".into()),
        recovery_secret: Some(generate_recovery_secret()),
        ..Default::default()
    }).await;

    assert!(result.is_ok());

    let config: ExportConfigJson = serde_json::from_str(
        &fs::read_to_string(output_dir.join("site/config.json")).unwrap()
    ).unwrap();

    assert_eq!(config.key_slots.len(), 2);
    assert_eq!(config.key_slots[1].kdf, "hkdf-sha256");
}
```

### 3) Integrity and Decrypt Roundtrip
- Decrypt payload chunks and verify DB bytes match expected fixture
- Tampering with a chunk fails authentication

### 4) Browser Integration (Playwright)
- Open page -> unlock -> search -> open conversation
- Verify virtual scroll and deep links
- Verify /stats and /settings routes

## Logging Requirements
- Each phase logs duration and PASS/FAIL
- JSONL log output for CI parsing

## Files to Create/Modify
- tests/e2e_pages.rs
- web/tests/e2e_pages.spec.js
- scripts/test-pages-e2e.sh

## Exit Criteria
1. All workflows pass end-to-end
2. Chunked payload + config.json validated
3. Decrypt -> search -> view path verified
4. Logs are detailed and CI-friendly

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2l5g","title":"[Task] Create connector_stress.sh E2E Script","description":"## Task: Create Connector Stress E2E Script

Create `scripts/e2e/connector_stress.sh` that stress-tests all connectors with malformed input.

### Script Purpose
Validate that connectors handle malformed input gracefully in a full E2E context:
- Index malformed session files
- Search indexed content
- Verify no crashes or data corruption

### Test Scenarios
1. **Truncated files** - Index sessions with truncated JSONL
2. **Invalid UTF-8** - Index sessions with invalid encoding
3. **Empty sessions** - Index empty session files
4. **Large sessions** - Index 10MB+ session files
5. **Concurrent indexing** - Index multiple malformed files simultaneously

### Script Structure
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source scripts/lib/e2e_log.sh

SCRIPT_NAME="connector_stress"
e2e_init "shell" "$SCRIPT_NAME"
e2e_run_start

# Setup: Create test fixtures
e2e_phase_start "setup" "Creating malformed test fixtures"
create_truncated_fixtures
create_invalid_utf8_fixtures
e2e_phase_end "setup"

# Test each scenario
for connector in claude codex cursor gemini aider cline amp opencode pi_agent goose; do
    e2e_phase_start "connector_$connector" "Testing $connector connector"
    
    e2e_test_start "truncated_${connector}" "connector_stress"
    if test_truncated_indexing "$connector"; then
        e2e_test_pass "truncated_${connector}" "connector_stress" "$duration"
    else
        e2e_test_fail "truncated_${connector}" "connector_stress" "$error_msg" "$duration"
    fi
    
    # ... more tests per connector ...
    
    e2e_phase_end "connector_$connector"
done

# Cleanup
e2e_phase_start "cleanup" "Removing test fixtures"
cleanup_fixtures
e2e_phase_end "cleanup"

e2e_run_end "$total" "$passed" "$failed" "$skipped" "$total_duration"
```

### Metrics to Emit
- `indexing_duration_ms` - Time to index malformed files
- `memory_peak_kb` - Peak memory during indexing
- `files_processed` - Count of files attempted
- `errors_caught` - Count of gracefully handled errors

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script created at `scripts/e2e/connector_stress.sh`
- [ ] All 10 connectors tested
- [ ] 5 test scenarios per connector
- [ ] JSONL output validates with schema
- [ ] No crashes on malformed input
- [ ] Output: `test-results/e2e/shell_connector_stress.jsonl`

### Verification
```bash
chmod +x scripts/e2e/connector_stress.sh
./scripts/e2e/connector_stress.sh
./scripts/tests/validate-e2e-jsonl.sh test-results/e2e/shell_connector_stress.jsonl
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:08:06.330427609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:12:52.628266126Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:12:52.628116498Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2l5g","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-6xnm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:08:29.238402093Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2ld2","title":"[Task] Opt 3.4: Benchmark parallel search (expect 4-8x on multi-core)","description":"# Task: Benchmark Parallel Search

## Objective

Measure the performance improvement from parallel search and document results across different core counts.

## Benchmark Protocol

### 1. Baseline (Sequential)
```bash
export CASS_PARALLEL_SEARCH=0
cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- vector_index_search_50k --save-baseline parallel_disabled
```

### 2. Parallel Enabled
```bash
unset CASS_PARALLEL_SEARCH
cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- vector_index_search_50k --save-baseline parallel_enabled
```

### 3. Compare Results
```bash
critcmp parallel_disabled parallel_enabled
```

## Expected Results

| Cores | Before (post-SIMD) | After Parallel | Speedup |
|-------|-------------------|----------------|---------|
| 1 | 10-15 ms | 10-15 ms | 1x (no improvement) |
| 4 | 10-15 ms | 3-4 ms | ~4x |
| 8 | 10-15 ms | 2-3 ms | ~6x |
| 16 | 10-15 ms | 1.5-2.5 ms | ~7x (diminishing) |

Speedup is sub-linear due to:
- Heap merge overhead
- Memory bandwidth saturation
- Rayon scheduling overhead

## Core-Scaling Test

Test with different core limits:
```bash
# Limit to 1 core
RAYON_NUM_THREADS=1 cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- vector_index_search_50k

# Limit to 2 cores
RAYON_NUM_THREADS=2 cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- vector_index_search_50k

# Limit to 4 cores
RAYON_NUM_THREADS=4 cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- vector_index_search_50k

# All cores (default)
cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- vector_index_search_50k
```

## Chunk Size Tuning

Test different chunk sizes:
```rust
// Modify PARALLEL_CHUNK_SIZE and benchmark
// Test: 256, 512, 1024, 2048
```

Expected: Optimal around 512-1024 for typical systems.

## Complete Optimization Chain Benchmark

Measure the full improvement from original to all optimizations:
```bash
# Original (no optimizations)
export CASS_F16_PRECONVERT=0
export CASS_SIMD_DOT=0
export CASS_PARALLEL_SEARCH=0
cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- vector_index_search_50k --save-baseline original

# All optimizations
unset CASS_F16_PRECONVERT
unset CASS_SIMD_DOT
unset CASS_PARALLEL_SEARCH
cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- vector_index_search_50k --save-baseline optimized

# Compare
critcmp original optimized
```

Expected: **20-30x improvement** (56ms → 2-3ms)

## Documentation Updates

After benchmarking, update:
1. PLAN_FOR_ADVANCED_OPTIMIZATIONS_ROUND_1__OPUS.md with actual results
2. Summary table showing full optimization chain
3. Consider adding to README.md

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] Sequential baseline measured
- [ ] Parallel enabled measured
- [ ] critcmp shows expected improvement
- [ ] Core-scaling test completed
- [ ] Full chain benchmark completed (56ms → 2-3ms)
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Dependencies

- Requires completion of Opt 3.3 (tests passing)","notes":"Completed parallel search benchmarking.
